---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-13"
---
```{r, echo=FALSE}
 library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
Blood_count <- read_excel("smears_analysis_r.xlsx")
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `total_cell` = `[Cells] c/ml`
  )
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Lympho%` = `Lymoph%`
  )

```

```{r, echo=FALSE}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Shark_Id` = `Sharks Id`
  )
```
```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(    
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id))
```
```{r}
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Shark_Id))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(car)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)

# --- 1. Check variance and normality ---
# Levene's Test
leveneTest(total_cell ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor, data = Blood_count, center = "median")

# Shapiro-Wilk Test
shapiro.test(Blood_count$total_cell)
```


```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)
library(DHARMa)
library(cld)

# --- 0. Fix group names and redefine factors ---

# CRITICAL FIX 1: Change "Contrôle" to "Static" in the base column
Blood_count$Groupe[Blood_count$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Static"

# CRITICAL FIX 2: Explicitly define the factor levels with the new name and order
Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
 mutate(
 week_factor = factor(week),
 # Redefine the factor using the corrected base column 'Groupe'
 Groupe_factor = factor(
        Groupe, 
        levels = c("Static", "Summer", "Winter") # Ensure 'Static' is first
    ),
 Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
)

# --- 1. Fit LMM including Sex ---
lmm_total <- lmer(total_cell ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
data = Blood_count)

summary(lmm_total)

# --- 2. Post-hoc comparisons: Groupe × Week ---
emms_total <- emmeans(lmm_total, ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor)
pairs_total <- pairs(emms_total, adjust = "tukey")

# Convert to data frame, add direction and significance
pairs_total_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_total) %>%
 mutate(
 Direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, "First > Second", "Second > First"),
 Significance = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
 )

# Export to Excel
write_xlsx(pairs_total_df, "TotalCell_Pairwise_Comparisons.xlsx")

# --- 3. Post-hoc comparisons: Sex within each Groupe × Week ---
emms_sex <- emmeans(lmm_total, ~ Sex_factor | Groupe_factor * week_factor)
pairs_sex <- pairs(emms_sex, adjust = "tukey")

# Convert to data frame, add direction and significance
pairs_sex_df <- as.data.frame(pairs_sex) %>%
 mutate(
 Direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, "Male > Female", "Female > Male"),
 Significance = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
)

# Export to Excel
write_xlsx(pairs_sex_df, "TotalCell_Sex_Pairwise_Comparisons.xlsx")

# --- 4. Plotting (Updated Colors) ---
# CRITICAL FIX 3: Update custom_colors definition
custom_colors <- c("Static" = "#D6C9AE", "Winter" = "#6F9FCC", "Summer" = "#D97A66")

# CRITICAL FIX 4: Ensure emms_df (not defined in this block) is created
# We'll create it here using the EMMs for plotting convenience
emms_df <- as.data.frame(emms_total)


p_total <- ggplot(emms_df, aes(x = week_factor, y = emmean, group = Groupe_factor, fill = Groupe_factor)) +
 geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), color = "black") +
 geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = emmean - SE, ymax = emmean + SE),
 width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), linewidth = 0.8) +
 scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
 labs(
 title = "",
 x = "Week",
 y = "Total cells count",
 fill = ""
 ) +
 theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p_total)

# --- 5. Overall Week Comparison ---
emms_week_main <- emmeans(lmm_total, ~ week_factor)

# 2. Perform pairwise comparisons on the marginal means
pairs_week_main <- pairs(emms_week_main, adjust = "tukey")

# 3. View the results (this is the "overall" test for Week)
summary(pairs_week_main)
```




```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(Rmisc)

# --- 0. Data Preparation ---

# CRITICAL FIX 1: Ensure the base column 'Groupe' uses "Static" instead of "Contrôle"
Blood_count$Groupe[Blood_count$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Static"

Blood_count <- Blood_count %>%
  mutate(
    # Ensure factors are set to the desired order: Winter, Static, Summer
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Winter", "Static", "Summer")),
    # Create the descriptive factor for weeks for better labels
    week_label = factor(paste("Week", week), 
                        levels = c("Week 4", "Week 6", "Week 8"))
  )

# Define the Antibody Color Palette (used for consistency)
antibody_colors <- c(
  "Static" = "#D6C9AE", 
  "Winter"  = "#6F9FCC",
  "Summer"  = "#D97A66" 
)


# --- 1. Summarize data: mean and SE per group and week ---
data_summary <- Rmisc::summarySE(
  Blood_count, 
  measurevar = "total_cell", 
  groupvars = c("Groupe_factor", "week_label")
)

# --- 2. Create the Final Bar Plot ---
p_total_cell_bar <- ggplot(
  data_summary, 
  aes(x = Groupe_factor, y = total_cell, fill = Groupe_factor)
) +
  # Bars for means (CRITICAL FIX: Added black border for definition)
  geom_bar(
      stat = "identity", 
      position = position_dodge(0.9), 
      color = "black" # Ensures a black line around each bar
    ) + 
  
  # Error bars for SE
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = total_cell - se, ymax = total_cell + se),
    width = 0.2, 
    position = position_dodge(0.9)
  ) +
  
  # --- Facet by Descriptive Week Label, moved to the BOTTOM ---
  facet_wrap(
    ~ week_label, 
    scales = "fixed", 
    strip.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  
  # Apply the Antibody Colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = antibody_colors) +
  
  # Labels and Theme Adjustments
  labs(
    title = "", # Removed Plot Title
    x = "", # Removed X-axis label
    y = "Mean Total Cell Count (± SE)",
    fill = "" # Removed Legend Title
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    # Remove the x-axis labels (Winter, Static, Summer) 
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    
    # Adjust facet strip to be at the bottom and clean up background
    strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(), 
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12), 
    
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines")
  )

print(p_total_cell_bar)
```
